input.background: It is important to develop a detailed plan of study early in the development of an investigation. One part of the plan is to assure adequacy of the sample size. Consider a test of H0: p1=p2 against Ha: p1≠p2 at α=0.05 with a power that is equal to 0.80. We predict that 20% of group 1 and 30% of group 2 will experience the outcome. If we recruit an equal number of individuals in each of the groups, how many individuals do we need to study?
input.question: How many individuals are needed in each group with continuity-correction?
